meaning: n.鍋囧畾,鍩烘湰鏉′欢,鍩烘湰鍘熺悊vt.瑕佹眰,鍋囧畾vi.瑕佹眰
examples: The universe encompasses all of space and time we know, so it is beyond the realm of the Big Bang model to postulate what the universe beyond our horizon is.
Therefore some scientists postulate that the Earth's earliest atmosphere, unlike that of today, was dominated by hydrogen, methane, and ammonia.
Let's postulate that she is a cook.
This was good enough to postulate that this bond would yield 6.5 percent in its outer years.
With the benefit of hindsight, it's a little easier to postulate why the votes went the way they were.
